Researchers University Limerick have found that applying pressure protein found tears whites yield electricity.
research team from Bernal Institute noticed that crystals lysozyme, model protein that plentiful whites birds well saliva, tears milk mammals produce electricity when pressed.
Their research paper been published October journal, Applied Physics Letters.
capacity make electricity applying pressure, known direct piezoelectricity, feature materials such quartz that change mechanical energy into electrical energy vice versa.
Such materials used range applications from resonators vibrators mobile phones ultrasound imaging deep ocean sonars.
Bone, wood tendon have been known possess piezoelectricity.
While piezoelectricity used around capacity generate electricity from this particular protein been explored.
extent piezoelectricity lysozyme crystals significant.
same order magnitude found quartz.
However, because biological material, non-toxic could have many innovative applications such electroactive anti-microbial coatings medical implants.
Crystals lysozyme easy create from natural sources.
"The high precision structure lysozyme crystals been known since 1965," said Structural Biologist Co-author Professor Tewfik Soulimane.
Professor Soulimane added, fact, second protein structure first enzyme structure that ever solved, first these crystals show evidence piezoelectricity".
Crystals gold-standard measuring piezoelectricity non-biological materials.
team shown that same approach taken understanding this effect biology.
This approach scientists have tried understand piezoelectricity biology using complex hierarchical structures such tissues, cells polypeptides rather than investigating simpler fundamental building blocks.
finding have reaching applications could result additional research area flexible electronics biomedical devices energy harvesting.
Future applications discovery include regulating discharge drugs body using lysozyme physiologically mediated pump that searches energy from surroundings.
Being naturally piezoelectric biocompatible, lysozyme serve alternative conventional piezoelectric energy harvesters, many which contain lethal elements such lead.
Professor Luuk Wielen, Director Bernal Institute Bernal Professor Biosystems Engineering Design expressed this breakthrough Researchers.
"The €109-million Bernal Institute ambition impact world basis science increasingly international context.
impact this discovery field biological piezoelectricity will huge Bernal scientists leading from front progress this field," said.
complete paper, 'The Direct Piezoelectric Effect Globular Protein Lysozyme', Aimee Stapleton, Mohamed Noor, John Sweeney, Vincent Casey, Andrei Kholkin, Christophe Silien, Abbasi Gandhi, Tewfik Soulimane Syed Tofail, published Applied Physics Letters.

conditioning system hasn't worked since bought (might need re-gassing).
